title: ‘Trump didn’t promise no wars’: Iran’s nuclear capabilities had to be disarmed for safety
description: Political Commentator Stephanie Hamill reinforced the idea that US President Donald Trump is only engaging in the war in the Middle East out of necessity.   “There’s definitely a split,” Ms Hamill told Sky News host Rowan Dean.  “The divide is concerning for Republicans who want to win the midterms and want to secure the next Presidential election.  “President Trump didn’t promise no war; he said that they didn’t want to do the endless wars and that they didn’t want to continue to intervene in foreign states, if you will.  “Sometimes you have to take these things on like a cancer before it spreads.”

title: ‘Everyone’s on edge’: Threats of a drone strike on California
description: Political Commentator Stephanie Hamill believes the threat to California should be taken seriously; however also believes the US is powerful enough to defend itself.   “Any threats like this should be taken seriously,” Ms Hamill told Sky News host Rowan Dean.  “It just shows you what we’re dealing with.   “Some of the things we have to keep in mind are homegrown terror.  “Everyone’s on edge.”

title: ‘Iran finally paying the price’: Trump is the first administration to take on the Regime
description: Political Commentator Stephanie Hamill praises US President Donald Trump for being the first president to take on the Iranian regime.   “President Trump is correct, Iran is finally paying a big price,” Ms Hamill told Sky News host Rowan Dean.  “After decades of being one of the world’s leading state sponsors of terrorism.  “We’ve heard presidents over the decades saying that they were going to contain Iran.  “Finally, we have a president who’s willing to do it.  “We had the administration that was ready to take them on head-on.”

title: ‘Mouthpiece for the regime’: CNN aired full statement of uninterrupted Iranian state propaganda
description: Sky News host Danica De Giorgio is appalled by CNN airing full statement of four minutes of uninterrupted Iranian state propaganda.   “Iran’s new Supreme Leader, who’s been missing in action, has vowed to enact revenge on the US and Israel,” Ms De Giorgio said.  “CNN actually aired the entire four-minute statement of uninterrupted Iranian state TV propaganda on its channel.  “So, when it’s not lying about ISIS-inspired terror attacks on home soil, it’s a mouthpiece for the regime.”

title: ‘Nail in the coffin’: US has already achieved its objectives in Iran
description: National Security Expert and Act for America Chair Brigitte Gabriel believes the US could end the war in the Middle East today and still have met all of its objectives.   “We have put the nail in the coffin with this operation,” Ms Gabriel told Sky News host Rowan Dean.  “America can stop its attacks today; we have already accomplished our mission.  “It’s just a matter of days or weeks before the Iranian people are able to take over their government.”

title: Iranian regime was looking like it was at the ‘end of the road’ earlier this year
description: Deakin University Professor of Global Islamic Politics Greg Barton says it was looking like the Iranian regime was at the “end of the road” earlier this year when protestors were killed.  Mr Barton told Sky News host Steve Price that it appeared the regime was “so brittle”.  “And had lost so much public support in an economy that’s in a perilous state.”

title: ‘Remarkably resilient’: Iranian regime able to function ‘without central control’
description: Deakin University Professor of Global Islamic Politics Greg Barton says the Iranian regime seems “very hierarchical” with their leadership positions.  Mr Barton told Sky News host Steve Price that it’s actually made to function with “31 separate divisions”.  “That can function without central control, so it’s actually remarkably resilient.”

title: Iranians ‘rally around the flag’ after being attacked
description: Deakin University Professor of Global Islamic Politics Greg Barton says the Iranian regime looks “stronger today” than it did a month ago.  Mr Barton told Sky News host Steve Price that there’s been a “rally around the flag” after the country was attacked.  “People understandably say, well better to stick with the regime we have and defend our country.”

title: US will ‘struggle’ to fight Iran beyond the next few weeks
description: Deakin University Professor of Global Islamic Politics Greg Barton says the US will “struggle” to fight beyond the next few weeks in Iran.  Mr Barton told Sky News host Steve Price that “shortages of supplies” of defensive missiles are making it difficult.  “Whereas this regime can probably throw out missiles, drones and carry on a fight for many months to come.”

title: South Australia's Premier warns the rise of populism threatens economic growth
description: South Australia Premier Peter Malinauskas is concerned about One Nation eating into the Labor vote in the outer suburbs.  “My concern is less about what happens to a particular vote than it is about the concern of the rise of populist politics more broadly,” Mr Malinauskas told Sky News host Caleb Bond.  “The risk of that from my perspective is that populism of itself is a threat to that economic growth.”

title: ‘A degree of civility’: Malinauskas calls out Aussie politicians for not having respect
description: South Australia Premier Peter Malinauskas calls out politicians who fail to show mutual respect and name-call each other.  “I’ve never won an argument by talking down to people,” Mr Malinauskas told Sky News host Caleb Bond.  “What we need now more than ever, particularly with the rise of populist politics, is to treat everyone with mutual respect.  “If someone’s got a grievance, there might be some legitimacy to it.  “We can come at it hopefully with a degree of civility.”

title: ‘Not that hard’: Freedom of religion is being threatened by political extremes
description: South Australia Premier Peter Malinauskas warns freedom of religion is being threatened by political extremes.  “We see these extremes seeking to denigrate one faith over the other,” Mr Malinauskas told Sky News host Caleb Bond.  “At the expense of the concept of freedom of religion.   “I just don’t think it’s that hard.”

title: Reports new Supreme Leader is in a coma and missing a leg
description: Sky News host Caleb Bond explains the reports new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is in the hospital.   “He is in intensive care at the Sina University hospital in Tehran after being critically injured in an airstrike,” Mr Bond said.  “We don’t know whether it’s the same one that killed his father.  “One or two of his legs have been cut off, his liver or stomach has also ruptured, and he is also apparently in a coma.”

title: Iranian’s can hold out for longer than predicted despite Trump ‘rolling in more aircrafts’
description: Strategic Analysis Australia Director Michael Shoebridge details the current state of the Middle East.   “If it’s true that the new Supreme Leader Khamenei’s son is incapacitated, it’s not affecting Iran’s conduct of the war,” Mr Shoebridge told Sky News host Caleb Bond.  “I think he’s [Trump’s] rolling in more aircraft.  “I think the Iranians, with their decentralised command and control and mobile missiles and drones, can keep doing what they’re doing despite air dominance.”

title: Chris Bowen blasted for ‘completely bungling’ diesel and petrol and av-gas crisis
description: Sky News host Steve Price calls out Minister for Climate Change and Energy of Australia Chris Bowen for failing to properly handle the diesel and petrol, and aviation gas crisis.  “Energy Minister Chris Bowen has completely bungled the Government's on-the-ground reaction to a diesel and petrol and av-gas crisis,” Mr Price said.   “It's staggering, truly staggering, to discover that with all the resources of the Federal public service and access to logistics experts across the nation, there was no plan in place.  “In the space of three days since his assurance, we don't have a problem to today, when he had to back up his useless move yesterday to release 100 million litres of so-called dirty petrol.”
